summary: Body Analyzer feature test for 3 new features (date pickers, share results, history viewer) + 2 polish items. Critical backend bug found and fixed: GET /api/body/history/{user_id} returned 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization. Frontend SSR bug found: After photo date defaults to '2026/2026' instead of '05/2026'. All other UI features working correctly.

critical.issue: Returned 500 Internal Server Error when user had existing analyses. MongoDB ObjectId (_id field) not JSON-serializable. Fixed by converting _id to str in the endpoint.

ui_bugs.issue: After photo date input defaults to '2026/2026' instead of '05/2026'. The browser correctly computes '05/2026' but the React SSR hydrated value is '2026/2026'. This is a server-side rendering (SSR) hydration mismatch. The server renders with '2026/2026' (possibly calling getFullYear() instead of getMonth() in the compiled SSR bundle), and React preserves the server-rendered value during hydration. The computed value in browser is: getMonth()=4, getFullYear()=2026 → '05/2026' but input shows '2026/2026'.

integration_issues.issue: When no user profile is loaded (user not logged in / profile not fetched), clicking 'Past Analyses' silently does nothing because loadHistory() returns early on !profile?.id. No user feedback (no Alert, no message). User gets no indication of why the history is not showing.

test_results_detail.screen_loads_without_errors: PASS - Body Analyzer screen loads correctly
test_results_detail.past_analyses_button_renders: PASS - Button renders at top of screen with clock icon
test_results_detail.past_analyses_api_called: PASS - API call made when user profile is loaded. Returns correct data after ObjectId fix.
test_results_detail.photo_dates_section: PASS - Two text inputs with 'Before photo' and 'After photo' labels, MM/YYYY placeholder
test_results_detail.after_photo_default: FAIL - Shows '2026/2026' instead of '05/2026' (SSR hydration bug)
test_results_detail.calculateTimePeriod_logic: PASS (code verified) - '01/2025' to '05/2025' correctly produces '4 months' via: totalMonths=(2025-2025)*12+(5-1)=4
test_results_detail.empty_history_state: PASS - 'No past analyses yet' displays correctly with analytics icon
test_results_detail.history_back_button: PASS - Back button in history view returns to upload form
test_results_detail.share_results_visibility: PASS - Share Results button NOT visible before analysis (only shows in results section)
test_results_detail.analyze_loading_state: NOT TESTED - Requires actual photos + active subscription to trigger
test_results_detail.history_endpoint_format: PASS (after fix) - Returns id, created_at, time_period, analysis fields correctly

action_items: HIGH: Fix After photo date SSR hydration issue - '2026/2026' shows instead of '05/2026'. Root cause: Server-side render in Expo Router produces wrong value for getDefaultAfterDate(). Suggested fix: Use useEffect to set afterDate on client side only: `useEffect(() => { setAfterDate(getDefaultAfterDate()); }, [])` with empty string as initial state to avoid SSR mismatch.
MEDIUM: Add user feedback when Past Analyses is clicked but user profile is not loaded. Currently silent failure. Add Alert or navigate to login.
LOW: Test Analyze button loading state 'Analyzing your transformation...' text - requires subscription access and photos, could not be automated.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: GET /api/body/history/{user_id} (server.py line 8166) returned raw MongoDB documents with ObjectId _id field → 500 error. Fixed by converting _id to str.

body-analyzer.tsx getDefaultAfterDate(): const now = new Date(); return String(now.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0') + '/' + now.getFullYear(); — code is logically correct but SSR renders '2026/2026'. Suggests Metro bundler SSR bundle may have a transpilation issue with this code, or SSR/hydration handling differs between server and client renders.
calculateTimePeriod() logic is correct: (a.y-b.y)*12 + (a.m-b.m) correctly computes months difference. Jan 2025 to May 2025 = 4 months. PASS.
Share Results button correctly only renders in the analysis results section (not in upload form) - PASS.
History items render with correct fields: progress score circle, date (formatted), time_period, summary text - PASS.

success_rate.backend: 100% (endpoint fixed, GET /body/history returns 200 with correct JSON)
success_rate.frontend: 80% (8/10 features verified - 1 SSR bug, 1 not testable without subscription)

seed_data_creation: Created test user: TEST_BodyUser (id: b9cc7b09-c5c2-4d8b-a60d-d84da84b5d38, email: testbody2@test.com). Used existing user d704bac8-fa54-4d5b-b984-cc17393c1244 for history tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Body Analyzer features tested. Critical fix applied: GET /api/body/history/{user_id} now returns 200 with correct JSON (was 500 due to MongoDB ObjectId). UI: After photo date SSR bug shows '2026/2026' instead of '05/2026' - needs useEffect fix. All other features: Past Analyses, empty history state, Back button, Share Results visibility, Photo Dates inputs all working. Backend history endpoint now correctly returns id, created_at, time_period, analysis fields. Analyze loading state ('Analyzing your transformation...') and Share Results button (only visible after analysis) could not be tested without subscription + photos.
rca_of_issue: CRITICAL BUG RCA: GET /api/body/history/{user_id} (server.py line 8166-8170). MongoDB's insert_one() adds _id (ObjectId) to the document dict. When to_list() returns documents, they include _id as ObjectId which FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize. Fix: iterate returned docs and convert _id to str. Pattern: same bug exists in /api/admin/free-access-list which had its own fix. Reproduction: call /api/body/history/{user_id} for any user with existing analyses. SSR BUG: After photo default '2026/2026' - browser evaluates new Date() correctly as month=5/year=2026='05/2026' but Expo SSR produces '2026/2026'. This suggests the SSR bundle transpiles getMonth() differently or uses a stale/cached build. Fix: wrap in useEffect with empty initial state to bypass SSR rendering of Date.
